channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australia tightens student visa approvals amid rising refusals
item.description: Australia's student visa system is experiencing a significant tightening, with refusal rates reaching their highest levels in roughly two decades. Recent data from the Australian Bureau of Statistics ...

item.title: Redondo Beach Pier evacuated due to police activity ahead of BeachLife Festival
item.description: Authorities in Redondo Beach, a coastal city near Los Angeles, ordered the evacuation of the Redondo Beach Pier on Sunday morning following ongoing police activity. The Redondo Beach Police Department...

item.title: Veteran Windsor Spitfires Players to Depart the Team After Long Tenure
item.description: The Windsor Spitfires, a long-established team in Ontario's junior hockey scene, are seeing several of their longtime players leave the roster. This marks a significant transition period for the organ...

item.title: Peter Obi exits ADC due to internal crises, may join new party with Kwankwaso
item.description: Peter Obi, former Governor of Anambra State, don officially announce say e dey leave the African Democratic Congress (ADC) because of wahala inside the party, including division, suspicion, and extern...

item.title: World War Two bomb safely detonated in Plymouth, residents evacuated temporarily
item.description: In Plymouth, bomb disposal experts successfully detonated a 250kg World War Two device, causing a significant explosion that was audible across the area. Authorities evacuated over 1,200 homes as a pr...

item.title: Sam Kerr becomes Chelsea’s all-time WSL top scorer in win over Leicester
item.description: Sam Kerr has described her latest milestone goal as the most special of her career after becoming Chelsea’s all-time leading scorer in the Women’s Super League (WSL). The Australian striker netted her...

item.title: Former Manchester United manager Alex Ferguson hospitalised after feeling unwell at Old Trafford
item.description: Alex Ferguson, the legendary former manager of Manchester United, was taken to hospital after feeling unwell before Sunday’s Premier League clash between United and Liverpool at Old Trafford. The 84-y...

item.title: Shakira Performs Live to 2 Million Fans at Rio's Copacabana Beach
item.description: Global pop superstar Shakira delivered a spectacular live performance at Rio de Janeiro's iconic Copacabana Beach, drawing a massive crowd of approximately 2 million fans. The concert took place under...

item.title: Peter Obi Urges Unity and Discipline After Joining NDC
item.description: Former Anambra State Governor, Peter Obi, don clear say he no dey desperate to become Nigeria President, Vice President, or even Senate President. Obi talk this one as e dey advise members of the Nige...

item.title: GPS-equipped key chain aids recovery of stolen Canberra BMW
item.description: On Anzac Day morning in Canberra, Binura Lakshan and his partner discovered their grey 2006 BMW 330i sedan missing from outside their Harrison home. Three masked men had allegedly broken in, taken the...

item.title: Interim Bondi royal commission findings highlight policing gaps despite no legal failures
item.description: The interim report from the royal commission into antisemitism and social cohesion has delivered its first set of findings following the deadly Bondi attack, where 15 people were killed during a Hanuk...

item.title: AI Innovations Aim to Improve Diagnosis and Treatment of Drug-Resistant Infections
item.description: Antibiotic resistance is an escalating global health crisis, responsible for over a million deaths annually and contributing to millions more. Traditional treatment often relies on physicians' judgmen...

item.title: UAE leaves OPEC+ to expand oil production, raising long-term market competition
item.description: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has decided to leave OPEC+ after nearly six decades, signalling a major shift in the global oil market. Frustrated by production quotas that limited its output below cap...

item.title: Clare Balding and Alice Arnold Move to Surrey for a Quieter Life After Two Decades in London
item.description: Clare Balding, the renowned British broadcaster, and her wife Alice Arnold have decided to relocate from west London to Esher in Surrey, seeking a slower pace of life and more space. After living for ...

item.title: Texas Startup Uses Robots to Construct Homes from Local Clay
item.description: A startup located south of Austin, Texas, is pioneering a novel approach to home construction by using robots to build houses from clay sourced directly on-site. The construction process begins with g...

item.title: Merz Stresses Need for Strong US-Germany Ties Despite Differences with Trump
item.description: In a recent interview, German political leader Friedrich Merz emphasized the significance of the transatlantic relationship between Germany and the United States, especially in light of past disagreem...

item.title: Dalit Women Sanitation and Daily Wage Workers Speak Up Against Workplace Exploitation
item.description: On Sunday, nearly 150 Dalit women, mostly from the Valmiki community, gathered at the Constitution Club of India in New Delhi to address issues of workplace exploitation. These women, employed as sani...

item.title: Wrestler Vinesh Phogat Confirms She is Among Six Complainants Against Brij Bhushan
item.description: In a significant disclosure, Indian wrestler Vinesh Phogat has publicly revealed that she is one of the six female wrestlers who had accused former Wrestling Federation of India chief Brij Bhushan Sha...

item.title: La Salle Secures Fourth Consecutive UAAP 3x3 Basketball Championship Against Ateneo
item.description: In a thrilling UAAP 3x3 basketball showdown, Luis Pablo led the De La Salle Green Archers to a decisive victory over their long-time rival, Ateneo, clinching their fourth consecutive championship in t...

item.title: Poilievre supports Ontario plan to expand Billy Bishop Airport amid political and community opposition
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre has voiced support for the Ontario government’s proposal to expand Toronto’s Billy Bishop Airport, arguing that the move would boost economic activity, improve con...
